name: Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. v. Estados Unidos Mexicanos
summary: Applied Twitter's secondary liability principles in the context of gun manufacturers, holding that selling lawful products to the general public on equal terms does not create aiding-and-abetting liability.
significance: Cited extensively for applying Twitter's aiding-and-abetting framework to product sellers, distinguishing between equivocal sales to the general public and targeted assistance to known wrongdoers.
